xBRIEFInfo.description: Scope xBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #68

plan.title: Add Dark Brown main color EQ preset

narratives.Description: Add Dark Brown main color EQ preset
narratives.LockedDecisions: Implement #68 (2026-07-14): (1) Catalog id `dark-brown`, label "Dark Brown". (2) DSP reuses Brown generator; darkness is EQ-only. (3) EQ: Brown through ≤125 Hz; 250 Hz = +2 dB; ≥500 Hz = −12 dB. (4) First-class entry in NOISE_COLORS / NOISE_COLOR_ORDER index 3. (5) Existing White/Pink/Brown unchanged.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Add a new main noise-color preset **Dark Brown**: same starting EQ shape as **Brown**, with a darker mid/high shelf.

Requested by Baba.

## Brown baseline (current)

Brown uses `eqCurveForColor("brown")` → `slopedEqCurve(6)` (6 dB/oct rolloff, pivot at band index 4.5), clamped to ±12 dB.

Bands (`EQ_BAND_FREQUENCIES` in `src/lib/eq.ts`):

| Hz | Brown gain (dB) |
|----|-----------------|
| 31 | +12 |
| 63 | +12 |
| 125 | +12 |
| 250 | +9 |
| 500 | +3 |
| 1000 | −3 |
| 2000 | −9 |
| 4000 | −12 |
| 8000 | −12 |
| 16000 | −12 |

Registry today: `NOISE_COLORS` / `EQ_ROLLOFF_DB_PER_OCTAVE_BY_COLOR` in `src/lib/noise.ts` (white / pink / brown only).

## Dark Brown (desired)

Copy Brown’s curve, then apply these overrides:

| Hz | Change |
|----|--------|
| 250 | **+2 dB** (was +9) |
| ≥500 (500, 1k, 2k, 4k, 8k, 16k) | **−12 dB** all |

Resulting Dark Brown curve:

| Hz | Dark Brown gain (dB) |
|----|----------------------|
| 31 | +12 |
| 63 | +12 |
| 125 | +12 |
| 250 | **+2** |
| 500 | **−12** |
| 1000 | **−12** |
| 2000 | **−12** |
| 4000 | −12 |
| 8000 | −12 |
| 16000 | −12 |

Bands below 250 Hz stay identical to Brown.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] New main color preset **Dark Brown** appears alongside White / Pink / Brown in the color UI.
- [ ] Selecting Dark Brown snaps the 10-band EQ to the curve above (and applies it to the audio graph via the existing preset→snap path).
- [ ] Brown (and other existing colors) are unchanged.
- [ ] Worklet / color registry / tests updated so Dark Brown is a first-class color (or an explicit documented EQ-only preset if product chooses that shape — prefer matching how Brown is registered today).
- [ ] Unit coverage asserts Dark Brown’s per-band gains match the table (especially 250 Hz = +2 and all ≥500 Hz = −12).

## Notes

- Gain floor is already `EQ_GAIN_MIN_DB = -12`; the ≥500 Hz shelf is at the legal minimum.
- Implementation likely touches `src/lib/noise.ts`, worklet color order (`public/worklets/noise-dsp.js` / `NOISE_COLOR_ORDER`), UI in `NoisePlayer`, and related tests — exact DSP generator for “dark brown” can reuse Brown’s generator with the custom EQ curve if a distinct algorithm is not needed.

items.title: New main color preset **Dark Brown** appears alongside White / Pink / Brown in the color UI.
Selecting Dark Brown snaps the 10-band EQ to the curve above (and applies it to the audio graph via the existing preset→snap path).
Brown (and other existing colors) are unchanged.
Worklet / color registry / tests updated so Dark Brown is a first-class color (or an explicit documented EQ-only preset if product chooses that shape — prefer matching how Brown is registered today).
Unit coverage asserts Dark Brown’s per-band gains match the table (especially 250 Hz = +2 and all ≥500 Hz = −12).
